headline: New Mexico shelters receive $2M boost for cats and dogs
meta_description: Seventeen animal shelters across New Mexico will receive nearly $2 million to improve care and sheltering of domestic cats and dogs.
summary_short: Seventeen animal shelters across New Mexico will receive nearly $2 million to improve the care and sheltering of domestic cats and dogs.
body:
Seventeen animal shelters across New Mexico will receive nearly $2 million to improve the care and sheltering of domestic cats and dogs, according to the New Mexico Department of Finance and Administration.

The funding represents a significant investment in animal welfare across the state, providing resources that shelters urgently need to expand their capacity and enhance conditions for vulnerable pets. With nearly $2 million distributed among 17 facilities, shelters will be able to upgrade infrastructure, improve medical care, and expand their sheltering programs to serve more animals in need.

The initiative underscores New Mexico's commitment to strengthening the network of animal care providers that work tirelessly to protect and rehome domestic animals throughout the state.
quick_facts: 17 animal shelters across New Mexico receiving funding
Nearly $2 million total distributed for improvements
Focus on domestic cats and dogs care and sheltering
Funding from New Mexico Department of Finance and Administration
